reviewer: new-demo build wave (runnability proof first, then headless Chrome real download+inference on index + all 4 ladder pages on one persistent profile, auto-init-on-cached probe, mobile+desktop overflow matrix, light/dark screenshots)

rubric.evidence: Runnability proven BEFORE building (headless Chrome, transformers.js@3.7.5, WASM, q8): MgpstrForSceneTextRecognition + MgpstrProcessor + RawImage classes all registered (typeof 'function'); onnx-community/mgp-str-base builds and model(inputs).logits = [char_logits[1,27,38], bpe_logits[1,27,50257], wp_logits[1,27,30522]]; processor.batch_decode(logits) -> {generated_text, char_preds, bpe_preds, wp_preds, scores}; per-word inference ~740 ms. Headless build verify: fresh profile -> Download (~155 MB) -> ready -> Read produced 'coffee' (fusion 100.0%), 0 console errors, 0 net failures. All inference off the main thread in worker.js.
rubric.notes: MGP-STR is a single forward pass with three parallel heads (char/BPE/WordPiece) + A^3 multi-granularity fusion — NOT autoregressive like TrOCR. Distinct family (mgp-str) from the built trocr/donut/nougat OCR.

rubric.evidence: Output shape + semantic sanity confirmed on every page against known sample words: index/basics read 'coffee' from the COFFEE crop; practical Read-all returned a7x9k2 / parking / coffee / stop / ticket (5/5 correct); wild read the serif-italic 'vintage' even rotated 12deg; multimodel DBNet detected 7 regions and MGP-STR read books/open/daily/inside/coffee at 98-100% and the slightly-clipped CORNER box as 'cornerb' at an honest 73%. The 'See inside' shows all three head predictions (char/bpe/wp) with a ✓ on the fused winner plus the per-character char-head confidence trace read straight from char_logits.
rubric.notes: image-to-text task template ('produces a caption/description that matches the image content') fits: here the output is a single recognised WORD + a fusion confidence, and each head's own reading.

rubric.evidence: conformance declares-model-id=onnx-community/mgp-str-base (linked to its card), declares-task=image-to-text, declares-backend=WASM/WebAssembly, declares-quantisation=q8, inference-off-main-thread (new Worker + worker.js). At-a-glance names the exact model, the real API surface (MgpstrForSceneTextRecognition + MgpstrProcessor, not the plain image-to-text pipeline), the [char,bpe,wp] logits shape, and the ViT-encoder->3-head->fusion architecture — no invented API.
rubric.notes: The page explicitly explains MGP-STR is NOT the default image-to-text pipeline and shows the real 3-line MgpstrForSceneTextRecognition + MgpstrProcessor + batch_decode call.

rubric.evidence: conformance shared-auto-init-loader=pass — every page loads MGP-STR through createModelLoader (backed by lib/model-cache.js), no hand-rolled Load button. Verified on a persistent profile: index fresh-profile showed the honest 'download-required' state + 'Download model (~155 MB)' button; after download, BASICS re-navigation auto-initialised to 'ready' with NO Download button present (auto-init-on-cached), and the multimodel loader showed 'Model ready' + 'Clear cached model' on the cached visit. transformers.js owns transformers-cache; the loader/model-cache scan finds '/onnx-community/mgp-str-base/'.
rubric.notes: The multimodel page keeps MGP-STR as its createModelLoader model and lazy-loads the tiny DBNet detector on first Detect.

rubric.evidence: conformance loads-clean=pass (0 console errors on load across index + 4 ladder pages), live-status-region=pass (role=status aria-live=polite on every runStatus + the loader status), errors-on-the-page=pass. Download progress streams from the worker progress_callback into the shared loader progress bar; inference failures surface in the .status.err line; the loader offers Retry on a failed init and a Clear-cached-model control. Fresh-profile load shows the honest Download state; cached load auto-inits.
rubric.notes: runs-without-webgpu-fallback: WASM-only (requiresWebGPU:false) — runs everywhere, verified on the no-GPU headless runner; no WebGPU-only gate.

rubric.evidence: Every control exercised headless and is labelled + keyboard-operable: sample thumbs (role=button, tabindex, Enter/Space), the drag-to-crop canvas + Reset-selection chip, file dropzone + input, Read / Add this word / Read all samples / Clear list / Detect & read buttons, the Copy controls, and the wild-page Rotate <input type=range> (drove value=12 via input event -> re-read). buttons-have-names=pass, images-have-alt=pass. Practical 'Read all samples' iterates the sample set through the worker; the copy-all writes the recognised list to the clipboard.
rubric.notes: Sliders/crop re-run a single bounded off-thread forward pass; no dead or decorative controls.

rubric.evidence: Every recognised word, confidence, granularity row and per-character trace comes from the on-device model run — no canned values. Distinct, correct-per-image results (coffee / a7x9k2 / parking / stop / ticket / vintage, and the 7-region multimodel read) and the honest low-confidence 'cornerb' (73%) on a clipped box prove the output tracks the real fused logits, not a placeholder.
rubric.notes: Sample word/scene images are generated for this demo (rendered text, no real brands/people).

rubric.evidence: conformance images-have-alt=pass, buttons-have-names=pass, live-status-region=pass. Sample images carry descriptive alt; the crop/detection canvases have aria-label (the multimodel canvas label updates with the detected-word count); the three-granularity comparison and the recognised-word batch are real DOM (a .gran-table of rows and an <ol> list) so screen readers and find-in-page reach the words + confidences as text — the accessible fallback for canvas content. The char-trace confidence bars expose an aria-label percentage. Status uses role=status aria-live=polite.
rubric.notes: Applied the expose-canvas-content guide: canvas visualisations are backed by real text (word list, granularity rows) rather than pixels-only.

rubric.evidence: conformance responsive-desktop=pass, responsive-mobile=pass. Measured document horizontal overflow = 0 px at 360x740 DPR3 on index + all 4 ladder pages (an initial 68 px overflow from the At-a-glance table's unbreakable code token MgpstrForSceneTextRecognition was fixed in place with table-layout:fixed + overflow-wrap:anywhere on the cells — a targeted CSS fix, no rewrite). Desktop 1280x900 screenshots reviewed: the two-column image|controls layout uses flex:1 1 <basis> + min-inline-size:0 and stacks on mobile; canvases are inline-size:100% block-size:auto; the granularity grid uses minmax(0,1fr); dark + light both legible.
rubric.notes: css-layout guidance applied: flex 1 1 basis + min-inline-size:0 to prevent overflow; wide code block scrolls inside its own overflow-x:auto pre (design-system).

rubric.evidence: All inference AND the per-position softmax / char-trace / per-head confidence post-processing run in a dedicated module Web Worker (worker.js); the main thread only renders DOM rows. Per-word inference ~740 ms on the headless WASM runner with no main-thread long task during interaction; the practical batch and the multimodel per-region loop await each off-thread call so the UI stays responsive.
rubric.notes: Guidance applied: break-up-long-tasks / schedule-tasks-by-priority (inference + the softmax/argmax/geo-mean post-processing off the main thread).

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive two column image and controls without horizontal overflow flexbox mobile
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use flexbox with flex:1 1 <basis> and min-inline-size:0 on flex items containing wide/unbreakable content so they shrink instead of overflowing; use gap + flex-wrap; size media with inline-size:100% block-size:auto; make tables shrink with table-layout:fixed + overflow-wrap:anywhere.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: Applied — the image/controls columns use flex:1 1 <basis> with min-inline-size:0 and stack on narrow viewports; the At-a-glance table uses table-layout:fixed + overflow-wrap:anywhere so its long code tokens break instead of forcing 68 px of overflow; verified 0 px document overflow at 360x740 on all five pages.

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low move inference off main thread web worker break up long tasks
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move long main-thread JavaScript (model inference and dense per-element loops) off the main thread into a worker; keep the main thread free for input/repaint.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: Applied — MGP-STR inference and all post-processing (per-position softmax over the 38/50257/30522 vocabularies, the char-head argmax trace, and the per-head geometric-mean confidence) run in a module worker; the page only builds DOM rows. No blocking loop during interaction.

guidanceConsulted.query: expose canvas visualization content to screen readers accessible text alternative
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Canvas content is invisible to assistive tech; provide an accessible representation (role=img + aria-label and a real text list/DOM of the content).
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: Applied (fallback strategy) — the crop/detection canvases carry aria-label (updated with the detected-word count on multimodel); the recognised words, the three-granularity comparison, and the per-character confidences are real DOM (an <ol> list and .gran-table rows with aria-labelled bars) that screen readers and find-in-page can reach, rather than pixels-only.

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible loading progress error states live region announce keyboard
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce loading/progress/ready/error in an accessible live region (role=status aria-live); every control needs an accessible name and keyboard operation.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: Applied — status lines use role=status aria-live=polite; sample thumbs are role=button with Enter/Space handlers; every button has a text name; the rotate control is a labelled <input type=range>. Verified buttons-have-names + images-have-alt pass.

openQuestions: MGP-STR reads ONE word per crop, so a multi-word box (e.g. the storefront 'SINCE 1998') is read as a single concatenated token; a follow-up could split detector boxes on internal whitespace before recognition.

MGP-STR's charset is a-z + 0-9 (lowercased); it cannot represent punctuation, accents or scripts beyond Latin — worth surfacing as an explicit capability note when a crop contains out-of-charset glyphs.

followUpGoals.goal: Add a live-camera ladder demo (lib/capture-ux.js, user-initiated) that boxes and reads a single word from a webcam frame for assistive capture.
Add a detect -> per-box whitespace split -> MGP-STR pass so multi-word detector regions are read as separate words in the multimodel pipeline.

summary: New demo: on-device scene-text RECOGNITION via MGP-STR (onnx-community/mgp-str-base, MIT) run through MgpstrForSceneTextRecognition + MgpstrProcessor in a Web Worker. Reads a single cropped WORD and predicts it three ways at once (character / BPE / WordPiece), fusing them — the three-granularity vote + the per-character char-head trace are the 'see inside'. Index + 4 ladder pages (basics single word, practical batch code/label reader, wild stylised+rotated with a live rotate control, multi-model DBNet->MGP-STR full scene OCR), all verified with real in-browser inference (proven runnable first), auto-init-on-cached, clean console, and 0 px mobile+desktop overflow. Distinct from the built TrOCR/Donut/Nougat (autoregressive line/doc OCR). Sample word/scene images are generated for this demo (no real brands/people).
